title: Teaching my mother to read English
summary: Reflect on the experience of teaching a parent to read simple English, exploring a change in family roles.
topic: Teaching a parent to read English
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about teaching an older family member a new skill (reading, using a phone, riding a bike). Describe what you teach, how often you do it, how they feel, and how the roles between you change. Use present simple and 'because' to explain why it is hard or rewarding.
hintStarters: Every Sunday afternoon, my mother and I…
At first she was shy because…
It feels strange because now I am…
sampleText: Every Sunday afternoon, my mother and I sit at the kitchen table with an English book. She is sixty years old, and she never learned English at school. I teach her the letters and the sounds first. At first she was shy because she does not want to make a mistake in front of her son. I tell her that mistakes are normal, and I am very patient. We start with simple words like 'milk', 'door', and 'rice'. She reads them slowly and repeats each word three times. Sometimes she laughs because her mouth feels strange with the new sounds. We also read the labels on food in the kitchen because it helps her every day. Now she can read short notes and small signs in the street. She is very proud, and I am proud too. It feels strange because now I am the teacher and she is the student. For many years she taught me everything. Today I give a little of that back, one quiet Sunday at a time.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chưa bao giờ học tiếng Anh ở trường
các chữ cái và âm thanh

sampleAnnotations.phrase: make a mistake in front of her son
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mắc lỗi trước mặt con trai mình
lặp lại mỗi từ ba lần

sampleAnnotations.phrase: now I am the teacher and she is the student
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bây giờ tôi là giáo viên và bà ấy là học trò
bà ấy đã dạy tôi mọi thứ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: one quiet Sunday at a time
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: từng buổi Chủ nhật yên tĩnh một

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Subject + verb agreement (she / I)
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Với 'she' động từ thêm -s: 'she reads', 'she repeats', 'she laughs'. Với 'I' động từ giữ nguyên: 'I teach', 'I tell'. Hãy chú ý đổi đúng theo chủ ngữ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: With 'she' the verb takes -s: 'she reads', 'she repeats', 'she laughs'. With 'I' the verb has no -s: 'I teach', 'I tell'. Match the verb carefully to the subject.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'because' for cause and reason
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'because' để giải thích lý do: 'she was shy because she does not want to make a mistake'. Sau 'because' luôn có một mệnh đề đầy đủ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'because' to explain a reason: 'she was shy because she does not want to make a mistake'. After 'because' there is always a full clause.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi chiều Chủ nhật, mẹ tôi và tôi ngồi ở bàn bếp với một cuốn sách tiếng Anh. Bà ấy sáu mươi tuổi, và bà chưa bao giờ học tiếng Anh ở trường. Tôi dạy bà các chữ cái và âm thanh trước. Lúc đầu bà ngại ngùng vì bà không muốn mắc lỗi trước mặt con trai mình. Tôi nói với bà rằng mắc lỗi là chuyện bình thường, và tôi rất kiên nhẫn. Chúng tôi bắt đầu với những từ đơn giản như 'milk', 'door' và 'rice'. Bà đọc chúng chậm rãi và lặp lại mỗi từ ba lần. Đôi khi bà cười vì miệng bà thấy lạ với những âm mới. Chúng tôi cũng đọc nhãn trên thực phẩm trong bếp vì nó giúp bà mỗi ngày. Bây giờ bà có thể đọc những ghi chú ngắn và những biển nhỏ ngoài đường. Bà rất tự hào, và tôi cũng tự hào. Cảm giác thật lạ vì bây giờ tôi là giáo viên còn bà là học trò. Trong nhiều năm bà đã dạy tôi mọi thứ. Hôm nay tôi trả lại một chút điều đó, từng buổi Chủ nhật yên tĩnh một.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes your Sunday habit most naturally?
options: Every Sunday afternoon, my mother and I sit at the kitchen table.
Every Sunday afternoon, my mother and I sits at the kitchen table.
Every Sunday afternoon, my mother and I sitting at the kitchen table.
Every Sunday afternoon, at the kitchen table my mother and I sit it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'my mother and I' is plural, so the verb takes no -s: 'sit'.
mcQuestions.hint: Is the subject singular or plural?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'She ___ each word three times.'
options: repeat
repeats
repeating
repeated

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'because' correctly to explain her shyness?
options: She was shy because of she does not want to make a mistake.
She was shy because want not to make a mistake she does.
She was shy because she does not want to make a mistake.
Because she was shy a mistake she does not want to make.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' is followed by a full clause with subject and verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes what you teach, with the right verb form?
options: I teaches her the letters and the sounds first.
I teaching her the letters and the sounds first.
I teach her first the letters and the sounds her.
I teach her the letters and the sounds first.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'I' the verb takes no -s: 'I teach'.
mcQuestions.hint: What ending does the verb take with 'I'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes her reading most naturally?
options: She reads them slowly and repeats each word three times.
She read them slowly and repeats each word three times.
She reads them slow and repeat each word three times.
She reads slowly them and three times each word repeats.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'reads' and 'repeats' agree with 'she', and 'slowly' is the correct adverb.
mcQuestions.hint: Check both verb endings and the adverb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains why you read food labels?
options: We read the labels on food because of it helps her every day.
We read the labels on food because it helps her every day.
We reads the labels on food because it help her every day.
We read the labels on food because every day her it helps.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'We read' has no -s, and 'because it helps her' is a correct reason clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Check 'We' agreement and the clause after 'because'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the new role between you?
options: Now I am the teacher and she are the student.
Now I is the teacher and she is the student.
Now I am the teacher and she is the student.
Now the teacher I am and the student she is now.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I am' and 'she is' are the correct forms of 'be'.
mcQuestions.hint: Match 'be' to each subject: I / she.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the past clearly?
options: For many years she teach me everything.
For many years she teached me everything.
For many years everything she taught me it.
For many years she taught me everything.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes her pride most naturally?
options: She is very proud, and I am proud too.
She is very proudly, and I am proud too.
She very proud is, and I am proud too.
She is very proud, and I am proud also too as well.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'proud' is the correct adjective after 'is', and the sentence is clean.
mcQuestions.hint: Use the adjective, not the adverb, after 'is'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best closes the reflection?
options: Today I gives a little of that back, one quiet Sunday at a time.
Today I give a little of that back, one quiet Sunday at a time.
Today I give a little of that back, one quiet Sundays at a time.
Today a little of that back I give, one quiet Sunday at a time it.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'I' the verb is 'give' (no -s), and the closing reads naturally.

criticalThinkingQuestion: When a child becomes the teacher of a parent, what does each person have to give up, and what do they both gain?
